_comment: Reproducible record of vendored third-party code. Re-fetch from `source`, verify sha256, and the vendored tree is byte-reproducible. Keep in sync when bumping a dependency.

vendored.name: three
vendored.notes: Official minified ESM build, used as-is (no custom rollup trim: three's WebGLRenderer is monolithic, so tree-shaking saves little). three.module.min.js is a THIN re-export of three.core.min.js (the bulk) — BOTH files are required; core.min has no further imports. Pulled from the npm registry tarball because jsdelivr/unpkg had incomplete mirroring of this version's core file. Serves both Path B (magic-window) now and Path A (WebXR, renderer.xr) later. GLTFLoader + other addons not yet vendored — add when content markers need model loading.

vendored.name: js-aruco2
vendored.license: MIT (per file; see LICENSE.txt for upstream attributions: BSD ArUco/OpenCV, the LGPL AForge entry is an algorithm reference for posit2, not vendored code)
vendored.notes: ARUCO_MIP_36h12 (the spec's mandated dictionary) is inline in aruco.js and is its default — the separate dictionaries/*.js are NOT vendored. posit1 = original POSIT (DeMenthon, what the upstream sample uses); posit2 = coplanar POSIT (better for a single flat marker). Both return the dual-candidate POS.Pose (best + alternative) the §5.2 disambiguation consumes. Loaded as classic scripts (globals AR/CV/POS/SVD); our ESM detector wrapper reads those globals.
